report.title: `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` parser drops spaces: `+` is never substituted back to a space
report.summary: `UrlEncodedForm::parse` in `hotaru_http/src/util/form.rs` and the query-string parser in `RequestPath::from_string` (`hotaru_http/src/message/http_value.rs`) treat `+` as a literal `+`. Per the WHATWG URL spec and the `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` form-submission rules, a `+` on the wire must decode to a space — browsers emit `+` for every space in `<form method="POST">` bodies and in URL query strings. The body parser also percent-decoded `%XX` but skipped the plus-to-space step; the query parser did no decoding at all.

impact: Any HTML form field containing a space is silently corrupted: a user typing `Hello world` is stored, echoed, or persisted as `Hello+world`, and the original space is unrecoverable downstream (database writes, template rendering, search indexing). Query strings are worse — neither `+` nor `%XX` was decoded, so `?q=Hello+world` and `?q=Hello%20world` both surfaced to handlers as their raw on-the-wire bytes. Affects every endpoint reached via `req.form().get(...)` or `req.query(...)`.

behavior_diff.expected: For a POST body `greeting=Hello+world&math=1%2B1`, `form.get("greeting") == Some("Hello world")` and `form.get("math") == Some("1+1")`. For `GET /search?q=Hello+world`, `req.query("q") == Some("Hello world")`.
behavior_diff.actual: Body parser returned `Some("Hello+world")` and `Some("1+1")` (the `%2B` happened to look correct only because `+` was also left untouched — a literal `+` typed by the user and a `+` representing a space were indistinguishable). Query parser returned the raw on-the-wire string, e.g. `Some("Hello+world")` or `Some("Hello%20world")`, with no decoding performed.

status.fix: Three coordinated changes: (a) `hotaru_lib/src/url_encoding.rs` adds `decode_form_url_owned()` — substitutes `+` to space first, then percent-decodes, so `%2B` correctly survives as a literal `+`; (b) `hotaru_http/src/util/form.rs` — `UrlEncodedForm::parse` now decodes both key and value with `decode_form_url_owned`; (c) `hotaru_http/src/message/http_value.rs` — `RequestPath::from_string` now form-decodes query-string keys and values (previously it did no decoding at all). The encode side already percent-encoded spaces as `%20`, which is valid in both URL and form contexts, so no change was needed there.
verification: cargo test -p hotaru_http --lib util::form::tests — 6 new tests cover: `+` → space, `%20` → space, `%2B` → literal `+`, `+` in key, mixed multi-pair body, and non-ASCII UTF-8 (`%C3%A9` → `é`). All pass.
